skill.name: brand-landingpage
skill.description: Brand-first landing page designer — interviews the user to discover brand identity (adjectives, colors, typography, shape language), then generates and iterates on a polished landing page via Stitch with deployment-ready HTML output. Preferred over frontend-design for standalone landing/marketing pages where the user hasn't established visual direction yet.
skill.summary: Brand-first landing page designer that interviews users about brand identity, then generates polished landing pages via Stitch.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: All 296 static analyzer findings are false positives from pattern matching on Markdown formatting, hex color codes, documentation URLs, and platform command references in instructional text. The skill is a legitimate design tool that guides brand interviews and generates landing pages via the Stitch SDK. No actual code execution, data exfiltration, credential theft, or malicious intent was found. The skill reads reference files, writes HTML and metadata locally, and calls the Stitch SDK — all behaviors consistent with its stated purpose of landing page design.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE (confidence: 0.98). 142 locations flagged for Ruby/shell backtick execution are all Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `create_project`, `open`, `xdg-open`). These are instructional text referencing SDK method names and platform commands in documentation, not executable shell code. No actual code execution or injection vector exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hex color codes flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE (confidence: 0.99). Over 100 locations flagged for weak cryptographic algorithm are hex color codes (#4F46E5, #DC2626, #1E3A5F, etc.) used in design system documentation and color mapping tables. These are CSS color values for the landing page designer, not cryptographic hashes. No crypto code exists in any skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs and setup instructions flagged as threats
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE (confidence: 0.97). URLs at SKILL.md:256-257 are Stitch SDK documentation links (stitch-design.ai/docs). The .env reference at SKILL.md:41 is SDK setup guidance. The cmd /c start at SKILL.md:176 is a cross-platform browser-opening instruction. No SAM database reference exists at SKILL.md:70 or anywhere — the static analyzer misidentified text in the workflow diagram. All are legitimate documentation content.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown code formatting mistaken for command execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 142 instances of backtick-delimited text (e.g., `create_project`, `xdg-open`, `zip -r`) as shell command execution. These are all Markdown inline code spans used for formatting SDK method names, platform commands, and file paths within instructional documentation. The skill instructs an AI agent to perform these actions; the backticks are formatting, not execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Color hex values mistaken for cryptographic hashes
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged hex color codes (#4F46E5, #DC2626, #1E3A5F, #16A34A, etc.) throughout color mapping tables and design documentation as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. These are standard CSS hex color values used in a design system reference, not cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Heuristic combination triggered by aggregate false positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The CRITICAL heuristic finding DANGEROUS COMBINATION: Code execution + Network + Credential access was triggered because the static analyzer detected external_commands (from Markdown backticks), network (from documentation URLs), and sensitive patterns (from .env setup instructions). Since all component detections are false positives, the aggregated heuristic is also a false positive.

content.user_title: Design Brand Landing Pages with AI-Guided Brand Discovery
content.value_statement: Many developers need a landing page but have not defined their brand colors, fonts, or visual style. This skill guides you through a focused brand interview and generates a polished, deployment-ready landing page that matches your product identity.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through a structured brand discovery interview across product, brand feel, and visual preference phases
Translates brand adjectives and preferences into a complete Stitch design system with fonts, colors, and shapes
Generates desktop and mobile landing pages using the Stitch SDK with iterative refinement
Supports design iteration through variants, targeted edits, and structured design feedback collection
Produces a deployment-ready HTML bundle with design tokens, documentation, and deployment checklist
Persists project state in a metadata file so interrupted sessions can resume from where they left off
limitations: Requires a Stitch account and API key for design generation
Cannot embed user-provided images or logos directly into generated pages
Limited to single landing pages and marketing sites, not multi-page applications or dashboards
Design generation takes 1 to 3 minutes per iteration and depends on Stitch SDK availability

use_cases.title: Developer Launching a Side Project
use_cases.description: A developer has built a tool but needs a landing page to promote it. They have no brand direction. The skill interviews them about their product and target users, then generates a polished landing page.

use_cases.title: Startup Founder Creating a Product Marketing Page
use_cases.description: A founder needs a landing page for their new SaaS product. The skill helps define the brand voice through guided questions and produces a conversion-focused page with clear CTAs.

use_cases.title: Open Source Maintainer Building a Project Homepage
use_cases.description: An open source maintainer wants a homepage that communicates their project's value to potential users and contributors. The skill generates a page with code snippets, features, and community calls to action.
use_cases.target_user: Open source maintainers and developer advocates

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landing page for my project.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with no brand direction ready for the guided interview

prompt_templates.title: Project with Basic Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a landing page for [project name], a [tool type] that helps [target users] [solve specific problem].
prompt_templates.scenario: User who knows their product but needs help with visual direction

prompt_templates.title: Project with Brand Preferences
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a landing page for [project]. My brand feels [adjective] and [adjective]. I want a [light/dark] theme with [color] accents.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who has thought about brand feel and wants to skip straight to visual design

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Design Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a [desktop/mobile] landing page for [project name] targeting [specific users]. Brand adjectives: [adj1], [adj2], [adj3]. Primary CTA: [action]. Preferred font style: [modern/traditional]. Shape language: [sharp/rounded]. Include sections for hero, features, [code demo/testimonials/pricing], and footer.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user providing a complete design brief for faster generation

output_examples.input: Create a landing page for Railtrack, an open-source deployment tool for backend engineers
output_examples.output: Brand interview questions about product details, target users, and CTA. After interview, generates a desktop landing page with code snippet section, feature cards, and deployment bundle.

output_examples.input: I want a dark theme with a bold and technical feel, using indigo accents and sharp corners
output_examples.output: Translates preferences into a design system with GEIST headlines, SPACE_GROTESK body text, indigo primary color, and ROUND_FOUR sharp radius. Generates matching landing page HTML.

output_examples.input: Show me some different layout options for the hero section and let me compare them
output_examples.output: Generates multiple variant HTML files and opens them in browser tabs. Highlights one distinguishing feature per variant for side-by-side comparison.

best_practices: Complete the full brand interview before generating designs. The interview is where most of the skill's value comes from.
Save all generated HTML versions to track design evolution and enable rollback to earlier iterations.
Translate CSS-level feedback into design intent language when communicating with Stitch tools.
anti_patterns: Skipping the brand interview produces generic templates that lack personality and brand alignment.
Overloading generation prompts with too many sections degrades output quality. Limit to 5 or 6 sections.
Retrying slow generation calls before checking completion status wastes API resources and may cause duplicate screens.

faq.question: Do I need a Stitch account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a Stitch API key from the Stitch dashboard to generate landing page designs. The skill helps you set it up during Phase 0.

faq.question: Can this skill build multi-page websites?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for single landing pages and marketing sites. It cannot build multi-page applications, dashboards, or documentation sites.

faq.question: Can I use my own images and logos in the generated landing page?
faq.answer: Stitch generates designs from text prompts and cannot embed uploaded images. Your original files are saved in the output bundle for manual integration into the final HTML.

faq.question: How long does it take to create a landing page?
faq.answer: The brand interview takes about 5 minutes. Each design generation takes 1 to 3 minutes. Most users complete a landing page in 15 to 30 minutes total.

faq.question: What if I already have brand colors and fonts defined?
faq.answer: Share your existing brand assets during the interview. The skill uses them directly in the design system instead of suggesting new ones.

faq.question: Can I resume a session if I get interrupted?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill saves all project state to a metadata file. When you restart, it detects the saved state and resumes from where you left off.
